Title: Rice NICOTIANAMINE SYNTHASE 2 expression improves dietary iron and zinc levels in wheat

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential micronutrients for human nutrition; iron deficiency causes anemia and reduced cognitive development, fatigue and low productivity; zinc deficiency causes stunted growth, decreased immunity and increased risk of respiratory infections
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primarily grain/endosperm-specific in transgenic wheat at 18 DAF; two lines also showed leaf expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in wheat; qRT-PCR; ICP-OES metal profiling in whole grain and flour; single-copy selection by Southern blot
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases iron and zinc content in wheat grain and flour, but less strongly than OsNAS2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endosperm-targeted PvFERRITIN expression increased grain iron to 42.0-61.9 μg/g DW versus 38.5 μg/g in control. 2. Flour iron reached up to 33.6 μg/g DW and zinc increased in both whole grain and flour. 3. PvFERRITIN alone improved mineral levels without major greenhouse growth penalties, but did not outperform OsNAS2.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major penalties for plant height or 1000-grain weight; some variation in days to flowering and tiller number
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for wheat biofortification, especially as an endosperm iron-storage strategy, but less effective than OsNAS2 alone in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Combined expression with OsNAS2 did not show synergy in wheat; PvFERRITIN expression level did not clearly correlate with iron content
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse, 16 h light/22 °C and 8 h dark/18 °C; grains analyzed 6 weeks after flowering

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential micronutrients for human nutrition; iron deficiency causes anemia and reduced cognitive development, fatigue and low productivity; zinc deficiency causes stunted growth, decreased immunity and increased risk of respiratory infections
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nicotianamine biosynthesis / mugineic acid-family phytosiderophore pathway / metal chelation and translocation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Nicotianamine production supplying deoxymugineic acid-mediated iron transport
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes biosynthesis of nicotianamine from S-adenosyl-L-methionine, supplying precursor for deoxymugeneic acid formation and metal chelation/transport
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool facilitating iron and zinc uptake, long-distance transport, and grain loading
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step/preferred engineering target for grain iron and zinc biofortification in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed strongly in leaves and also in grains; much higher in leaves than grains in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in wheat; qRT-PCR; ICP-OES metal profiling in whole grain and flour; correlation of transgene expression with iron content; single-copy selection by Southern blot
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Greatly increases iron and zinc in wheat grain and flour, reaching nutritionally significant levels and outperforming PvFERRITIN alone or stacked lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive OsNAS2 expression increased whole-grain iron to 59.6-93.1 μg/g DW and flour iron to 30.1-53.3 μg/g DW. 2. OsNAS2 also increased whole-grain zinc to as high as 140.6 μg/g DW and flour zinc to 94.1 μg/g DW, exceeding dietary targets. 3. OsNAS2 expression in leaves correlated with iron accumulation and was concluded to be the preferred strategy for wheat iron biofortification.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most lines were phenotypically similar to control; some lines flowered earlier, and 1000-grain weight was increased in some lines and decreased in line 81; other metals including Cu, Mn, and Mg also increased
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for biofortification breeding and transgenic engineering; preferred single-gene strategy for nutritionally relevant iron and zinc enhancement in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states NAS catalyzes nicotianamine biosynthesis and that nicotianamine is a precursor of deoxymugeneic acid required for long-distance iron translocation; combined stacking with PvFERRITIN was not synergistic in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse, 16 h light/22 °C and 8 h dark/18 °C; grains analyzed 6 weeks after flowering
